''[[Dheepan]]'' | host = [[Lambert Wilson]] | number = 19 (In Competition) | date = 13 – 24 May 2015 | website = {{URL|http://www.festival-cannes.com/en.html}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2014 Cannes Film Festival|2014]] | next = [[2016 Cannes Film Festival|2016]] }} The '''68th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 13 to 24 May 2015.{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en.html |title=Cannes Film Festival |access-date=5 October 2014 |work=Cannes |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150905111239/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/festival.html |archive-date=5 September 2015 }} [[Coen brothers|Ethan and Joel Coen]] were the Co-Presidents of the Jury for the main competition,{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/61245.html |title=Joel and Ethan Coen to preside over the Jury of the 68th Festival de Cannes |access-date=20 January 2015 |work=Cannes |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160826194705/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/61245.html |archive-date=26 August 2016 }} marking the first time that two people co-chaired the jury.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-30894984 |title=Coen brothers to jointly head Cannes jury |access-date=20 January 2015 |work=BBC News |date=20 January 2015 |archive-date=20 January 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150120215856/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-30894984 |url-status=live }} Since the Coen brothers each received a separate vote, they were joined by seven other jurors to form the customary nine-juror panel. French filmmaker [[Jacques Audiard]] won the {{Lang|fr|[[Palme d'Or]]|italic=no}}, the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[Dheepan]]''. During his speech, Audiard stated that "receive a prize from the Coen brothers is something pretty exceptional. I'm very touched".{{cite news |date=24 May 2015 |title=Cannes Palme d'Or awarded to French film Dheepan |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-32867228 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150524202005/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-32867228 |archive-date=24 May 2015 |access-date=25 May 2015 |work=BBC News}} French film director [[Agnès Varda]] was presented with the [[Honorary Palme d'Or]] at the festival's closing ceremony. She was the first female filmmaker to ever receive the award.{{cite web |title=A Palme d'honneur to Agnès Varda |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/61348.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150518093527/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/61348.html |archive-date=18 May 2015 |work=Cannes}} The festival's official poster featured Swedish actress [[Ingrid Bergman]], photographed by [[David Seymour (photographer)|David Seymour]]. The poster was chosen to pay tribute to Bergman for her contributions to films; she also served as the Jury President at [[1973 Cannes Film Festival]].{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/61281.html |title=It's poster time at the Festival de Cannes! |access-date=24 March 2015 |work=Cannes |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170105042552/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/61281.html |archive-date=5 January 2017 }} Once again, French actor [[Lambert Wilson]] was the host for the opening and closing ceremonies.{{cite web |title=Lambert Wilson to host ceremonies of the 68th Festival de Cannes |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/61274.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160826194709/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/61274.html |archive-date=26 August 2016 |access-date=12 March 2015 |work=Cannes}}{{cite web |date=16 April 2015 |title=Cannes 2015: Fassbender and Blanchett head for festival |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2015/apr/16/cannes-2015-line-up-michael-fassbender-cate-blanchett-oscars |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150416143237/http://www.theguardian.com/film/2015/apr/16/cannes-2015-line-up-michael-fassbender-cate-blanchett-oscars |archive-date=16 April 2015 |access-date=16 April 2015 |work=The Guardian}}{{cite news |date=16 April 2015 |title=Cannes Film Festival line-up announced |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-32334982 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150417045203/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-32334982 |archive-date=17 April 2015 |access-date=16 April 2015 |work=BBC News}} The edition was infamously marked by artistic director [[Thierry Frémaux]] pledge to celebrities to abstain from taking selfies on the red carpet. While he did not have the powers to ban the pictures from the red carpet altogether, Frémaux urged celebrities to resist the temptation.{{cite news |
